description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Register two children for an affordable July summer Bible camp morning session
tasks.description: Find a summer Bible camp in July 2025 for ages 7–10 that costs under $200 per child, and complete registration for two children (ages 7 and 10) in a morning session using a pay-at-check-in option.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Events" link in the main navigation menu.
On the Events page, open the "Event Type" dropdown and select "Summer Bible Camp".
Use the date range picker to set the start date to July 1, 2025 and the end date to July 31, 2025, then apply the date filter.
Open the "Age Range" filter and select the option that covers "7–10 years".
In the sort dropdown, select "Price: Low to High".
From the filtered list, click the first camp event card that shows a per-child price under $200.
On the camp detail page, click the "Register" button.
In the registration form, enter information for two children with ages 7 and 10, and for each child select "Morning Session" from the session dropdown.
In the payment section, select the "Pay at Check-In" option and click the "Submit Registration" button.

tasks.name: Set up a $35 monthly donation for Children's Bible Story Kits
tasks.description: Create a recurring monthly pledge of $35 designated to the "Children's Bible Story Kits" fund, including a dedication in honor of "Grandma Mary" and choosing a non–online payment option.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Donate" button in the header navigation.
On the Donate page, click the "Monthly" tab to switch from one-time to recurring donations.
In the preset amounts area, select the "$35" monthly amount option.
Open the "Designate my gift" dropdown and choose the "Children's Bible Story Kits" fund.
Check the box labeled "I'd like to cover the processing fee" (or similar wording) so that fees are included in your pledge.

In the dedication section, toggle on "Dedicate this gift", choose "In honor of" from the dedication type dropdown, and type "Grandma Mary" in the honoree name field along with a short message in the message text area.
Fill in the donor information fields with a first name, last name, email address (e.g., "donor@example.com"), and mailing address.
In the payment method section, choose the option labeled "Bill me by mail" or "Send me instructions to pay later" instead of entering card details.
Click the "Complete Monthly Pledge" button to finalize the $35 recurring donation setup.

tasks.name: Save three Easter crafts for ages 6–8 that take under 30 minutes into a collection
tasks.description: Find craft activities for ages 6–8 related to Easter, limited to activities that take 30 minutes or less, save the first three results, and group them into a new collection called "Easter 6–8 Crafts".
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Resources" item in the main navigation, then select the "Crafts & Activities" subcategory from the dropdown.
On the Crafts & Activities page, use the age filter to select "6–8" and in the topic filter select "Easter".
Adjust the activity duration filter so that the maximum time is set to "30 minutes" and apply the filter.
In the sort dropdown above the results list, choose "Most Downloaded".
From the filtered results, click the "Save" or "Add to Favorites" icon on the first three different craft activity cards that appear in the list.
Click the "My Saved Resources" or favorites icon in the site header to open your saved resources page.
On the saved resources page, click the "Create New Collection" button, enter the name "Easter 6–8 Crafts" into the collection name field, and click "Create".
Use the checkboxes next to the three saved Easter crafts to select them, then open the "Add to Collection" dropdown and choose the "Easter 6–8 Crafts" collection.
In the sidebar or collections list, click on the "Easter 6–8 Crafts" collection name to open the collection and view those three activities together.

tasks.name: Add two Fruit of the Spirit curriculum sets under $25 for different age groups to the cart
tasks.description: Purchase one "Fruit of the Spirit" curriculum set for ages 3–5 and one for ages 9–11, each priced under $25, by filtering, sorting by highest rating, and adding the top-rated qualifying item for each age group to the cart.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Shop" link in the main navigation.
On the Shop page, in the category sidebar, select the "Curriculum Sets" category.
Use the search bar above the product grid to type "Fruit of the Spirit" and submit the search.
In the filters, select the "Ages 3–5" age group and adjust the price filter so that the maximum price is set to "$25".
In the sort dropdown, choose "Customer Rating: High to Low".
From the filtered results, click the first product card shown and on the product detail page click the "Add to Cart" button.
Use the "Back to Results" link or browser back button to return to the Fruit of the Spirit search results.

In the filters, uncheck "Ages 3–5" and check "Ages 9–11" while keeping the maximum price at "$25", then ensure the sort remains "Customer Rating: High to Low" and click the first product card in the new results.
On this second product’s detail page, click the "Add to Cart" button, then click the cart icon in the header and select "View Cart" to open the cart page with both curriculum sets.

tasks.name: Apply for an in-person Sunday morning children’s volunteer role within 10 miles of ZIP 30309
tasks.description: Find an in-person volunteer opportunity that involves working directly with children on Sunday mornings at a location within 10 miles of ZIP code 30309, and submit a volunteer application for that specific role.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Get Involved" option in the main navigation and select "Volunteer" from the dropdown.
On the Volunteer page, use the "Serve Type" filter to select "In-Person" and in the "Role Focus" filter select "Work Directly with Children".
In the location search field, enter the ZIP code "30309" and in the distance dropdown choose "Within 10 miles", then apply the location filter.
In the availability filters, select the "Sunday" checkbox and choose the "Morning (8am–12pm)" time slot.
From the filtered list sorted by distance, click the first volunteer opportunity card that meets these criteria.
On the opportunity detail page, click the "Sign Up to Volunteer" button.
In the volunteer application form, enter your full name, email address, and phone number, and select a campus or site from the campus dropdown if available.
In the availability section of the form, select a specific Sunday morning service time (for example, check the box for "Sunday 9:00am service").
Scroll to the bottom of the form and click the "Submit Volunteer Application" button.

tasks.name: Create a four-week April 2025 parables lesson series for ages 5–7
tasks.description: Plan four distinct lesson plans about Jesus’ parables for ages 5–7, assign them to a new series titled "April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)", and arrange them into a four-week order for the four Sundays in April 2025.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Hover over the "Resources" menu and click on the "Lesson Plans" option.
On the Lesson Plans page, use the age filter to select "Ages 5–7" and in the topic filter open "Teachings of Jesus" and check the "Parables" option.
In the calendar or date filter section, select the month "April 2025" so the planner shows four Sundays for that month.
Click the "Create New Series" button, type "April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)" into the series name field, and click "Save".
From the list of filtered parables lesson plans, click the "Add to Series" button on the first four different lesson plan cards in the list.
In each "Add to Series" popup, select the "April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)" series from the dropdown and confirm the addition.
Click the "My Lesson Series" or "Series Planner" link in the header or sidebar to open your series overview.
On the "April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)" series page, drag and drop the four lessons to arrange them as Week 1, Week 2, Week 3, and Week 4 in the desired order.
Click the "Save Series Order" button to store the four-week April 2025 lesson sequence.

tasks.name: Subscribe to a weekly parent newsletter focused on family devotions for ages 8–10
tasks.description: Sign up for the parent newsletter using an email address, selecting weekly frequency and topics related to family devotions and prayer for children ages 8–10.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Scroll to the footer and click the "Parent Newsletter" link.
On the newsletter signup page, type "parent@example.com" into the "Email Address" field.
In the "Children's Ages" multi-select or checkbox list, select ages "8", "9", and "10".
In the "Topics I'm Interested In" section, check the boxes for "Family Devotions" and "Helping Kids Pray".
Open the "Email Frequency" dropdown and choose the "Weekly" option.
In the communication preferences section, select the "Email only" option and check the box labeled "Send me age-specific ideas for ages 8–10".
Click the "Sign Up for Newsletter" button to submit your preferences.

tasks.name: Sponsor a child aged 6–9 with a $30–$40 monthly pledge focused on reading and Bible stories
tasks.description: Find a child sponsorship profile for a child aged 6–9 who is interested in reading and Bible stories, with a monthly sponsorship amount between $30 and $40, and complete the sponsorship setup using a bill-by-mail payment option.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Get Involved" menu item and choose "Child Sponsorship" from the dropdown.
On the Child Sponsorship page, use the "Monthly Amount" slider or fields to set the minimum amount to "$30" and the maximum amount to "$40".
In the age filter, select the "6–9 years" option, and in the interests filter check the box for "Reading & Bible Stories".
In the sort dropdown above the child profiles, select "Longest Waiting" so that children who have waited longest appear first.
From the filtered list, click the first child profile card that appears.
On the child’s profile page, click the "Sponsor for $35/month" (or another amount between $30 and $40 shown) button.
In the sponsorship form, enter your full name and email address, and in the "Start Date" date picker select the first day of the next month.
In the payment options section, choose the "Bill me by mail" or "Invoice by mail" option instead of entering any card information.
Click the "Confirm Sponsorship" button to complete the sponsorship setup.
